scope_note: QA Haramein Scaling Diagonal Cert family [218]: Haramein-Rauscher-Hyson 2008 (Scale Unification: A Universal Scaling Law for Organized Matter, Proc. Unified Theories Conf., Budapest) Table 1 of six canonical scale points (Big Bang/Planck, Atomic, Stellar Solar, Galactic G1, Galactic G2, Universe) encoded as integer (b,e) exponent pairs in (log10 R cm, log10 nu Hz) with canonical offset b = log R + 33, e = log nu + 33 sit on a QA fixed-d hyperbola d = b + e = constant (d = 10 = log c decades + 2*offset-correction; equivalently R * nu = c is the diagonal equation). On this hyperbola, four structural segment-ratios on 2D Euclidean distances in (log R, log nu) space approximate phi^2 or 1/phi^2 (phi = (1+sqrt(5))/2), identified verbatim in Haramein Fig 2b labels. Ratios are computable from integer quadratic forms on (delta b, delta e): (25^2+25^2)/(16^2+15^2) = 1250/481 ~ phi^2; (6^2+7^2)/(4^2+4^2) = 85/32 ~ phi^2; (2^2+3^2)/(4^2+4^2) = 13/32 ~ 1/phi^2; (16^2+16^2)/(25^2+25^2) = 512/1250 ~ 1/phi^2 (all within 7%). Null test (N=200000 random 6-point slope-minus-1 placements, same four structural pair positions): p < 5e-6. This places Haramein's hierarchy in the Q(sqrt 5) = Z[phi] algebraic family (companion to [219] Fibonacci Resonance), on a fixed-d hyperbola (distinct from [217] Fuller VE b=e diagonal D_1). Primary source: Documents/haramein_rsf/scale_unification_2008.pdf (acquired 2026-04-13 via spacefed.com direct PDF). Theory note: docs/theory/QA_HARAMEIN_SCALING_DIAGONAL.md. Source: Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-13. Checks HSD_1+TABLE/FIXED_D/SEGMENTS/PHI_RATIOS/QUADRATIC/NULL/SRC/WITNESS/F; 1 PASS + 1 FAIL; self-test ok
